DSA Art. 26.summary: Very Large Online Platforms must provide meaningful information about the main parameters used in their recommender systems and any options for users to modify or influence those parameters.
DSA Art. 26.indicator_basis: When the same factual claim is reproduced across multiple accounts in a short window, especially across languages, the platform's recommender behaviour and the absence of mitigation may constitute a transparency failure.

DSA Art. 34-35.summary: VLOPs must identify and mitigate systemic risks stemming from the design and use of their services, including risks to civic discourse, electoral processes, and public security.
DSA Art. 34-35.indicator_basis: When high-reach political content semantically matches a fact-check published by an EDMO-affiliated organisation, AND the platform's own DSA Transparency Database filings show it has acted on similar claims elsewhere, the persistence of this content may indicate inadequate mitigation under Art. 35.

AI Act Art. 50.summary: Providers and deployers must ensure that AI-generated or AI-manipulated image, audio, video, or text content is marked as such in a machine-readable format and detectable as artificially generated. Deepfakes and AI-generated text on matters of public interest carry specific labelling duties.
AI Act Art. 50.indicator_basis: When a video carries reasonable indicators of AI involvement (uploader-declared in the description, AI-related hashtags, ClaimReview AI-generated tag, or third-party synthetic-media likelihood at or above the configured threshold) AND the platform has not surfaced the AI Act-mandated disclosure label, this indicates a potential platform-level disclosure failure. Narrative Radar does not itself classify content as AI-generated.
